title: swida × dubia
intro: Swida × dubia, also known as Guelder-rose or Canadian cranberrybush, is a hybrid deciduous shrub native to North America, resulting from the cross between the American cranberrybush (Viburnum opulus) and the Downy cranberrybush (Viburnum lentago). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Swida × dubia include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Swida × dubia can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall well-being and treat specific health conditions.
